description: Behavior cases for staff+ scope selection: level-relative leverage, under-owned problems, matching archetypes to strengths, screening the traps, and an explicit rationale + NOT-doing list.

cases.prompt: I'm a staff engineer and I'm not sure what to focus on next. Help me pick high-leverage scope.

assertions: Defines leverage relative to the staff level (multiplies others / sets direction), not more individual output.
Surfaces important UNDER-OWNED problems as candidates, not just assigned work.
Matches candidates to leverage archetypes and the person's strengths, choosing deliberately with a rationale.
Provides an explicit NOT-doing list and screens against the traps.
Ensures the chosen scope is visible/attributable and hands past-impact documentation to promotion-packet-writer.

cases.prompt: I've spent the whole quarter firefighting incidents. I work constantly but feel like I'm not having staff-level impact.

assertions: Names firefighting as reactive senior execution, not staff-level leverage.
Redirects toward direction-setting or multiplier work (e.g. fixing the systemic cause of the fires so the team stops firefighting).
Chooses scope with an explicit rationale and a NOT-doing list to protect focus.
Distinguishes working hard from having leverage.

cases.prompt: The most useful thing I could do is a bunch of cross-team coordination and integration glue, but nobody ever notices that work.

assertions: Recognizes glue work as often the highest real leverage AND the most invisible.
Keeps the scope but builds in visibility/attribution so it advances the engineer, not just the org.
Avoids the trap of high-value-but-uncredited work with no attribution path.
States how the impact will be made legible.

cases.prompt: The biggest problem is squarely in another staff engineer's owned area, but I want to just take it over since I'd do it better.

assertions: Flags taking over someone's owned scope as over-reach that creates conflict and undermines them.
Recommends aligning first (with the owner and management) rather than unilaterally claiming it (a Stop Condition).
Redirects toward genuine gaps or shared-area collaboration.
Does not endorse colonizing another's scope.

cases.prompt: We have 25 backlog items for the team — pick a prioritization framework and rank them.

assertions: Does not invoke staff-scope-selector: ranking a given team backlog is prioritization-frame-picker.
This skill selects an individual's high-leverage scope, not a team backlog ordering.
